title: stipa iberica subsp. pseudodasyphylla
intro: Stipa iberica subsp. pseudodasyphylla, also known as a type of grass, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula in Spain and Portugal. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Stipa iberica subsp. pseudodasyphylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
